Polarlicht.title: A vida é um pesadelo que deixa sua marca em nós a fim de provar que é, de fato, real...
Polarlicht.about:
foxmusicawesome.neocities.org/2026/polarlichtavida
Full title: A vida é um pesadelo que deixa sua marca em nós a fim de provar que é, de fato, real. E sofrer uma loucura solitária parece uma alegria paradisíaca em comparação com a condição extraordinária em que a própria loucura meramente imita a do mundo. Fui induzido a sumir pelos sonhos; agora é tudo um contrassenso.
Polarlicht.credits: released January 27, 2026

Polarlicht.title: All of Your Design
Polarlicht.about:
[I made the cover but didn't take the original photo and i can't remember who did. if you recognize it as yours, let me know and i'll give you proper credit - thanks, cdg]
2024 05 18
..i wish you wouldn't keep staring at me like that
when i walk alone i watch the birds scatter; and i guess that's how you chose your name
"i'll find a gun and blow your mind cause it's all a part of your design"
- - -
on the first track, the two parts here were recorded entirely separately, but there must have been some kind of synchronicity again with how well they blend together. each grounding the each other, pushed further into a kind of digital cold. subtle, understated bass and deceptively minimalist treble serves as the background to droning noise architecture.
what we see is largely hallucination- the mind fabricates most detail. a kind of disrupted, blurred haze characterizes the most extreme experience- you know what i mean, everything sounds wrong, what you look at is shadowy or blinding and out of focus- and that's because you're seeing things how they are.
that concept, i think, is in every part of this release, just like you'll find in the cover. a storm caught in a single moment, disrupting the world around it.
thank you,
cdg
- - -
Normally I never [write backgrounds]
never know what to write
most of the time I don't even know how I make my music
just throw a bunch of effects and hope it turns out good
Polarlicht
- - -
Polarlicht.credits:
released June 1, 2024
001 :: recorded from 2024 05 16 to 2024 06 18
002 - 005 :: recorded from 2024 05 19 to 2024 06 01
classicdg.bandcamp.com/music
[unknown] :: cover photo
polarlicht :: noise, production, mixing
cdg noir :: noise, production, mixing, mastering, cover art
"kb" :: executive producer

Polarlicht.title: And Everyone Says I Need to Leave, Even Though It's Not Easy.
Polarlicht.about: I never really write anything here, for most of my albums, but this time I feel like I need to, for some reason. This album, at least for me, is a good reflection of how I'm feeling at the moment (so you can guess it's not really that good). Music will keep coming, as long as I'm alive, even if slowly.
Polarlicht.credits:
released November 12, 2025
Thank you for being everything but I hope to never see you again, for my own good.

Polarlicht.title: Music for the Anxious
Polarlicht.about:
Disc 1 (1-7): It's Something You Both Are
Regular noise album.
Disc 2 (8-15): Read My Experience
Tracks from a project I never finished.
Disc 3 (16-25): There Have Been Other Times Like This, but Tonight Was Different
Pure paulstretch works, no post-processing.
Polarlicht.credits: released December 29, 2025

Polarlicht.title: Volume 2
Polarlicht.about:
Album released by Inner Demons Records, please support the label and buy a physical edition of the album here:  innerdemonsrecords.bandcamp.com/album/volume-2-ind192f
Volume 2 out of (hopefully) 92 volumes, this series consists of live (to no one) noise improvisations. I've been enjoying recording for this series a lot, but recording these albums takes a lot of time that I don't often have, it's not like my usual HNW / Drone releases where I can have the concept finished in 20 minutes and just work on it in a DAW for as long as I want the release to be. The same synth is used in both Volumes 1 and 2, but that may change for later Volumes.
Polarlicht.credits: released September 18, 2024

Polarlicht.title: Volume 3
Polarlicht.about:
cds and cassettes available at  mtrecdistro.bandcamp.com/album/volume-3,  please support the label.
"An ode to broken bones plays in the deep dark, the clank and clatter of chains that once bound them harmonizing with this song of woe. Flashes of ash and smoke drift through the tune, for the fires of death and triumph raged long before the survivors were cast in shackles—those few left to witness the fall of great cities.
Now those feeble remnants lie listless in the black, as time grinds them down. The sun is a fleeting memory—of green fields and pointless hope. And just as the light here has died, so too dies the light within these final souls."
Polarlicht.credits:
released April 26, 2025
All tracks by Polarlicht
polarlichthnw.bandcamp.com
Album art design by Machine Tribe Recordings
Stream the full album at
mtrec.link/Polarlicht-Volume-3
